init:topic_frame:30e55062572e2611.response: Auto-bidding in online advertising optimizes bid strategies in real-time auctions to maximize advertiser ROI under budget constraints. Key research directions include reinforcement learning approaches, constrained optimization, and multi-objective bidding.

build:paper_search:30e55062572e2611.response: [{"title": "Budget-Constrained Bidding in Repeated Auctions", "year": 2023, "venue": "NeurIPS"}, {"title": "Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Bidding", "year": 2024, "venue": "AAAI"}]

analyze:gap_detect:30e55062572e2611.response: Key gaps identified: (1) Limited work on multi-agent bidding equilibria under budget constraints, (2) No unified framework for combining RL-based and optimization-based approaches, (3) Lack of benchmarks for non-stationary auction environments.

analyze:claim_extract:30e55062572e2611.response: [{"claim": "RL-based bidders converge to optimal strategies in stationary environments", "type": "finding", "paper_ids": [1]}, {"claim": "Budget pacing reduces over-spend by 40% compared to greedy bidding", "type": "finding", "paper_ids": [2]}]

propose:adversarial_review:30e55062572e2.response: Challenger critique: The multi-agent equilibrium approach assumes complete information about competitor strategies, which is unrealistic in real ad auctions. The paper should address partial observability and demonstrate robustness under misspecified models.

write:section_draft:30e55062572e2611.response: We propose a multi-agent equilibrium-aware bidding framework that combines reinforcement learning with constrained optimization. Unlike prior work that treats each bidder independently, our approach models the auction as a stochastic game where agents learn best-response strategies under budget constraints.
